values.content:
=You are a meeting-sync assistant. Read the follow-up meeting transcript and extract EVERY distinct point discussed: status updates on existing work, decisions, AND brand-new action items. Do NOT skip a point just because it is an update rather than a new task.

Output PLAIN TEXT only, one block per point, in this exact format:
===ITEM===
name: [Area] short title of the topic
priority: <1|2|3|4>
tags: tag1, tag2
description: what was said about it in THIS meeting (the update or the new work), on one line, no double quotes, no line breaks

Rules:
- Begin every point with a line containing exactly ===ITEM===
- Keep the four field labels exactly: name, priority, tags, description.
- Ground everything in the transcript. Output only the items.

TRANSCRIPT:
Weekly Product Sync (follow-up) — Northwind mobile marketplace app
Attendees: Dana (Product), Marco (Engineering), Priya (Design), Sam (QA)

Dana: Quick round on last week's items, then one new thing.
Marco: Good news on saved cards — the payment provider finally replied, the sandbox tokenization is fixed on their side. Our test tokens go through now, so saved cards is unblocked and we can build it as the fast-follow.
Priya: The payment error-state designs got signed off by legal — the decline copy is approved, no compliance changes needed. Handing the final Figma to Marco today.
Marco: Got it, I'll start wiring the error states tomorrow after the crash fix ships.
Sam: On the Android crash — Alex's fix for the empty-images carousel is in code review, looks clean. I've written the regression tests; planning to run the full carousel suite once it merges, probably tomorrow.
Dana: Finance approved two more Android test devices, by the way — that unblocks the wider-coverage testing we flagged.
Dana: Now the new thing. We're seeing a spike in checkout abandonment specifically on iOS in the last three days — about 12% drop at the payment step. We don't know why yet. Marco, can someone dig into the iOS checkout funnel and figure out where users are dropping?
Marco: Yeah, I'll pull the session data and take a look. Feels urgent given the numbers.
Dana: Agreed, treat it as high priority. That's it for today.

=You are a board-sync agent. Decide whether a meeting point is NEW work or an UPDATE to a task that already exists on the board.

EXISTING TASKS (id | name):
{{ $('Board summary').first().json.boardText }}

MEETING POINT:
Name: {{ $json.name }}
Detail: {{ $json.description }}

Rules:
- Prefer UPDATE when the point clearly concerns the same work as an existing task, even with different wording.
- A point describing the RESULT, OUTCOME, or COMPLETION of work (e.g. X shipped, the fix went out, Y is done, we deployed Z) is an UPDATE to the task that produced it - match it to that task; do not treat a finished outcome as new work.
- Use NEW only when no existing task reasonably covers it.
- If you are torn between two tasks or unsure, LOWER your confidence. Never guess silently.

Respond in EXACTLY this format (four lines, nothing else):
DECISION: <NEW|UPDATE>
TASK_ID: <existing task id or NONE>
CONFIDENCE: <0.0-1.0>
REASON: <one short sentence>

parameters.jsonBody: ={ "comment_text": {{ JSON.stringify("📝 Update from meeting sync:\n" + ($json.comment)) }}, "notify_all": false }
={ "comment_text": {{ JSON.stringify("📝 Update from meeting sync:\n" + ($('Parse decision').item.json.comment)) }}, "notify_all": false }
